Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}}23rd year ({{jalink|804th year|Q29998}}) |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Akaishi Shrine/Ukishima Myojin |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = 1st Friday, Saturday, and Sunday of September |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Iwate|label=Shinto torii
Waki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Shigariwake-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Shiga Riwake Shrine'' (Shigariwake Jinja/Shikariwake Jinja, Shiga Riwake Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Iwate Prefecture|Q48326}}{{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886}}{{jalink|Shiwa Town|Q387336}} Sakuramachi. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. Also known as ``Akaishi Shrine'' and ``Ukishima Myojin''. It is known for being the northernmost Shikinai shrine. === Enshrined God === The following seven deities are enshrined. . * {{jalink|Kyotsu no Kami|Q1107286}} (Futunushi no Kami) * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchijin}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōki no Kami}} (Onamuji no Kami) * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona no Kami}} * {{jalink|Hoshokujin|Q2280323}} (Ukemochi no Kami) * {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahikokami}} * Funadama no Kami Among the deities enshrined, five of them, including Okami no Kami, are said to have been enshrined together in later generations. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine legend, in {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 23rd year ({{jalink|804|Q29998}}), {{jalink|Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979}} was appointed as the guardian deity of Tohoku development. . In the early part of the {{jalink|9th century|Q8083}}, the area was constructed with {{jalink|Shiva Castle|Q11491083}} ({{jalink|803 year|Q30388}}), Shiba District ({{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886}}) construction, {{jalink|Tokutan Castle|Q11489487}} construction ({ {jalink|811|Q29946}}) and other areas are being developed, and our company is also working to reward the pacification of {{jalink|Ezo|Q932574}} and to protect the area. {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. There are many examples of Shikinai-sha shrines in Ou being built by subsuming the landowner deity and inviting a central deity, but it is not clear whether this is the case for our company, and the origin of the divine name ``Shiga Riwaki'' is also uncertain {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. === Outline === The first appearance in the {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokukushi}} was in the second year of {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} ({{jalink|852 years|Q30383}}), the {{jalink|god of Shigari Wakejin}. The article states that Kana|Q11591025}} was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi, but judging from the content, it appears to have been a {{jalink|government shrine}} that had a divine rank even before Ninju {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. {{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} alink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886|Shiba District}}, it is described as "Shiga Riwake Shrine" and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1990}} is the only Shikinai shrine in Shiba County and the northernmost Shikinai shrine in Japan.After the collapse of the Ritsuryo system, there are no historical documents from the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, and its condition at that time is not known, but it appears to have fallen into such disrepair that one could even forget that it was a Shikinai shrine.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}} Shrine (Gods) | 1984}}, {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} At the beginning of {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}}, it was called ``Akaishi Daimyojin'' because Akaishi was {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. A legend about this red stone is that when the lord of {{jalink|Kosui-dera Castle}}, {{jalink|Akinao Shiba|Q11500879}}, passed through this area, {{jalink|Kitakami River|Q1348297}} There is a red stone at the bottom of the river, and the water waves are purple, so he wrote a poem that goes, ``Rather than Kefu, I would name it Shiwa, because the waves on the stones of this river resemble purple.''{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Because of this, the name of the district was ``Shinami-gun,'' and the name of the shrine was ``Akaishi Daimyojin,'' and it is said that the red stone that was salvaged is still enshrined within the shrine grounds.It is said that in the middle of the {{jalink|18th century|Q7015}}, the history of the Shikinai shrine was revived and the shrine building was rebuilt.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. {{jalink|Masumi Sugae, who traveled here in 1785|Q7756}} in {{jalink|Tenmei|Q1194121}} |Q11619285}}'s travelogue also mentions our company as Shigariwake Shrine, a Shikinai shrine {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. In addition, our company was revered by the {{jalink|Nambu clan|Q2975330}}, the lord of the {{jalink|Morioka clan|Q908854}}, and was dedicated with the name ``Nanbu Ichi no Miya'' {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. Early modern rituals were once carried out by Akaishiyama Hensho-ji Temple as {{jalink|Bettoji|Q11396755}}, and this Hensho-ji Temple had a temple territory of {{jalink|Keicho |Q1328185}} 30 koku were given in 7 years ({{jalink|1602|Q6727}}) {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. However, {{jalink|Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|Since the temple was abandoned in the Genwa}} years ({{jalink|1615|Q6784}}-{{jalink|1624|Q6821}}), the Tamura family of farmers took over for a while, and then Korin-ji Temple became the special charge {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}.After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in October of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), {{jalink|Modern company ranking system|Q712534}} |Q134917316}}, and was promoted to {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} on March 26, {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}13 ({{jalink|1924|Q2173}}). Furthermore, during this period, the shrine petitioned for promotion to Kokuheisha twice, once in 1908 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}) and again in 2nd year of Taisho ({{jalink|1913|Q2080}}). === God level === * August 7th, 2nd year of {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} ({{jalink|852 years|Q30383}}), the lower rank of the fifth rank (``{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905}}'') - Written as ``Shiga Riwakejin.'' === Precincts === The approach is lined with about 30 {{jalink|cherry blossom|Q871991}} tall trees. The variety is {{jalink|Azumahigan}}. The largest of these trees is 6 meters and 80 centimeters in circumference at the base, 11 meters and 5 centimeters in height, and is estimated to be about 500 years old. It is believed to be the oldest group of Higanzakura in the prefecture, and has been designated as a natural monument by Shiwa Town as the ``southern cherry tree.'' File:Shigariwake-jinja honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja haiden.JPG|House of worship File:Shigariwake-jinja Akaishi.JPG|Akaishi File:Shigariwake-jinja sakura.JPG|Cherry blossoms on the approach
(Shigariwake-cho designated natural monument) === Setsusha === There are three shrines within the precincts as follows. . * Sakashita Inari Shrine * Onofuji Inari Shrine * Akaishi Tenmangu Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Sakashita-inari-jinja.JPG|Sakashita Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Onofuji-inari-jinja.JPG|Onofuji Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Akaishi-tenmangu.JPG|Akaishi Tenmangu === Festival === * Donto Festival (January 15th)The description of the festival is based on the shrine history book. *Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Prayer Festival (Spring Festival) (April 29th) * Summer Purification Ceremony (June 30th) * Doll Festival (June 30th) * Reitaisai (Autumn Festival) (first Friday, Saturday and Sunday of September) === Cultural assets === === Shiwa Town Designated Natural Monument === * Cherry blossoms and Higanzakura group on the south side - Designated on March 25, 1975. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Iwate '岩手県紫波郡紫波町桜町字本町川原1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shigariwake-jinja}} [[JA:志賀理和氣神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17216962}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shigariwaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Main building of Shiga Riwaki Shrine (Akaishi Shrine).jpg|250px]]
Shrine building newly built in 2021 |Location = {{jalink|Iwate Prefecture|Q48326}}{{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886}}{{jalink|Shiwa Town|Q387336}}Sakuracho Honmachi Kawahara 1 |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Ketsunushi|Q1107286}}
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchijin}}
{{jalink|Okuninushi|Q276944|Okumi no Kami}}
{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukuhikomyojin}}
{{jalink|Hoshokujin|Q2280323}}
{{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahikojin}}
Funereishin |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Prefectural company}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}}23rd year ({{jalink|804th year|Q29998}}) |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Akaishi Shrine/Ukishima Myojin |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = 1st Friday, Saturday, and Sunday of September |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Iwate|label=Shinto torii
Waki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Shigariwake-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Shiga Riwake Shrine'' (Shigariwake Jinja/Shikariwake Jinja, Shiga Riwake Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Iwate Prefecture|Q48326}}{{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886}}{{jalink|Shiwa Town|Q387336}} Sakuramachi. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. Also known as ``Akaishi Shrine'' and ``Ukishima Myojin''. It is known for being the northernmost Shikinai shrine. === Enshrined God === The following seven deities are enshrined. . * {{jalink|Kyotsu no Kami|Q1107286}} (Futunushi no Kami) * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchijin}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōki no Kami}} (Onamuji no Kami) * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona no Kami}} * {{jalink|Hoshokujin|Q2280323}} (Ukemochi no Kami) * {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahikokami}} * Funadama no Kami Among the deities enshrined, five of them, including Okami no Kami, are said to have been enshrined together in later generations. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine legend, in {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 23rd year ({{jalink|804|Q29998}}), {{jalink|Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979}} was appointed as the guardian deity of Tohoku development. . In the early part of the {{jalink|9th century|Q8083}}, the area was constructed with {{jalink|Shiva Castle|Q11491083}} ({{jalink|803 year|Q30388}}), Shiba District ({{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886}}) construction, {{jalink|Tokutan Castle|Q11489487}} construction ({ {jalink|811|Q29946}}) and other areas are being developed, and our company is also working to reward the pacification of {{jalink|Ezo|Q932574}} and to protect the area. {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. There are many examples of Shikinai-sha shrines in Ou being built by subsuming the landowner deity and inviting a central deity, but it is not clear whether this is the case for our company, and the origin of the divine name ``Shiga Riwaki'' is also uncertain {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. === Outline === The first appearance in the {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokukushi}} was in the second year of {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} ({{jalink|852 years|Q30383}}), the {{jalink|god of Shigari Wakejin}. The article states that Kana|Q11591025}} was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi, but judging from the content, it appears to have been a {{jalink|government shrine}} that had a divine rank even before Ninju {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. {{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} alink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886|Shiba District}}, it is described as "Shiga Riwake Shrine" and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1990}} is the only Shikinai shrine in Shiba County and the northernmost Shikinai shrine in Japan.After the collapse of the Ritsuryo system, there are no historical documents from the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, and its condition at that time is not known, but it appears to have fallen into such disrepair that one could even forget that it was a Shikinai shrine.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}} Shrine (Gods) | 1984}}, {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} At the beginning of {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}}, it was called ``Akaishi Daimyojin'' because Akaishi was {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. A legend about this red stone is that when the lord of {{jalink|Kosui-dera Castle}}, {{jalink|Akinao Shiba|Q11500879}}, passed through this area, {{jalink|Kitakami River|Q1348297}} There is a red stone at the bottom of the river, and the water waves are purple, so he wrote a poem that goes, ``Rather than Kefu, I would name it Shiwa, because the waves on the stones of this river resemble purple.''{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Because of this, the name of the district was ``Shinami-gun,'' and the name of the shrine was ``Akaishi Daimyojin,'' and it is said that the red stone that was salvaged is still enshrined within the shrine grounds.It is said that in the middle of the {{jalink|18th century|Q7015}}, the history of the Shikinai shrine was revived and the shrine building was rebuilt.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. {{jalink|Masumi Sugae, who traveled here in 1785|Q7756}} in {{jalink|Tenmei|Q1194121}} |Q11619285}}'s travelogue also mentions our company as Shigariwake Shrine, a Shikinai shrine {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. In addition, our company was revered by the {{jalink|Nambu clan|Q2975330}}, the lord of the {{jalink|Morioka clan|Q908854}}, and was dedicated with the name ``Nanbu Ichi no Miya'' {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. Early modern rituals were once carried out by Akaishiyama Hensho-ji Temple as {{jalink|Bettoji|Q11396755}}, and this Hensho-ji Temple had a temple territory of {{jalink|Keicho |Q1328185}} 30 koku were given in 7 years ({{jalink|1602|Q6727}}) {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. However, {{jalink|Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|Since the temple was abandoned in the Genwa}} years ({{jalink|1615|Q6784}}-{{jalink|1624|Q6821}}), the Tamura family of farmers took over for a while, and then Korin-ji Temple became the special charge {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}.After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in October of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), {{jalink|Modern company ranking system|Q712534}} |Q134917316}}, and was promoted to {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} on March 26, {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}13 ({{jalink|1924|Q2173}}). Furthermore, during this period, the shrine petitioned for promotion to Kokuheisha twice, once in 1908 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}) and again in 2nd year of Taisho ({{jalink|1913|Q2080}}). === God level === * August 7th, 2nd year of {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} ({{jalink|852 years|Q30383}}), the lower rank of the fifth rank (``{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905}}'') - Written as ``Shiga Riwakejin.'' === Precincts === The approach is lined with about 30 {{jalink|cherry blossom|Q871991}} tall trees. The variety is {{jalink|Azumahigan}}. The largest of these trees is 6 meters and 80 centimeters in circumference at the base, 11 meters and 5 centimeters in height, and is estimated to be about 500 years old. It is believed to be the oldest group of Higanzakura in the prefecture, and has been designated as a natural monument by Shiwa Town as the ``southern cherry tree.'' File:Shigariwake-jinja honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja haiden.JPG|House of worship File:Shigariwake-jinja Akaishi.JPG|Akaishi File:Shigariwake-jinja sakura.JPG|Cherry blossoms on the approach
(Shigariwake-cho designated natural monument) === Setsusha === There are three shrines within the precincts as follows. . * Sakashita Inari Shrine * Onofuji Inari Shrine * Akaishi Tenmangu Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Sakashita-inari-jinja.JPG|Sakashita Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Onofuji-inari-jinja.JPG|Onofuji Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Akaishi-tenmangu.JPG|Akaishi Tenmangu Waki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Shigariwake-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Shiga Riwake Shrine'' (Shigariwake Jinja/Shikariwake Jinja, Shiga Riwake Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Iwate Prefecture|Q48326}}{{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886}}{{jalink|Shiwa Town|Q387336}} Sakuramachi. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. Also known as ``Akaishi Shrine'' and ``Ukishima Myojin''. It is known for being the northernmost Shikinai shrine. === Enshrined God === The following seven deities are enshrined. . * {{jalink|Kyotsu no Kami|Q1107286}} (Futunushi no Kami) * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchijin}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōki no Kami}} (Onamuji no Kami) * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona no Kami}} * {{jalink|Hoshokujin|Q2280323}} (Ukemochi no Kami) * {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahikokami}} * Funadama no Kami Among the deities enshrined, five of them, including Okami no Kami, are said to have been enshrined together in later generations. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine legend, in {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 23rd year ({{jalink|804|Q29998}}), {{jalink|Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979}} was appointed as the guardian deity of Tohoku development. . In the early part of the {{jalink|9th century|Q8083}}, the area was constructed with {{jalink|Shiva Castle|Q11491083}} ({{jalink|803 year|Q30388}}), Shiba District ({{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886}}) construction, {{jalink|Tokutan Castle|Q11489487}} construction ({ {jalink|811|Q29946}}) and other areas are being developed, and our company is also working to reward the pacification of {{jalink|Ezo|Q932574}} and to protect the area. {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. There are many examples of Shikinai-sha shrines in Ou being built by subsuming the landowner deity and inviting a central deity, but it is not clear whether this is the case for our company, and the origin of the divine name ``Shiga Riwaki'' is also uncertain {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. === Outline === The first appearance in the {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokukushi}} was in the second year of {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} ({{jalink|852 years|Q30383}}), the {{jalink|god of Shigari Wakejin}. The article states that Kana|Q11591025}} was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi, but judging from the content, it appears to have been a {{jalink|government shrine}} that had a divine rank even before Ninju {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. {{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} alink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886|Shiba District}}, it is described as "Shiga Riwake Shrine" and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1990}} is the only Shikinai shrine in Shiba County and the northernmost Shikinai shrine in Japan.After the collapse of the Ritsuryo system, there are no historical documents from the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, and its condition at that time is not known, but it appears to have fallen into such disrepair that one could even forget that it was a Shikinai shrine.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}} Shrine (Gods) | 1984}}, {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} At the beginning of {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}}, it was called ``Akaishi Daimyojin'' because Akaishi was {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. A legend about this red stone is that when the lord of {{jalink|Kosui-dera Castle}}, {{jalink|Akinao Shiba|Q11500879}}, passed through this area, {{jalink|Kitakami River|Q1348297}} There is a red stone at the bottom of the river, and the water waves are purple, so he wrote a poem that goes, ``Rather than Kefu, I would name it Shiwa, because the waves on the stones of this river resemble purple.''{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Because of this, the name of the district was ``Shinami-gun,'' and the name of the shrine was ``Akaishi Daimyojin,'' and it is said that the red stone that was salvaged is still enshrined within the shrine grounds.It is said that in the middle of the {{jalink|18th century|Q7015}}, the history of the Shikinai shrine was revived and the shrine building was rebuilt.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. {{jalink|Masumi Sugae, who traveled here in 1785|Q7756}} in {{jalink|Tenmei|Q1194121}} |Q11619285}}'s travelogue also mentions our company as Shigariwake Shrine, a Shikinai shrine {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. In addition, our company was revered by the {{jalink|Nambu clan|Q2975330}}, the lord of the {{jalink|Morioka clan|Q908854}}, and was dedicated with the name ``Nanbu Ichi no Miya'' {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. Early modern rituals were once carried out by Akaishiyama Hensho-ji Temple as {{jalink|Bettoji|Q11396755}}, and this Hensho-ji Temple had a temple territory of {{jalink|Keicho |Q1328185}} 30 koku were given in 7 years ({{jalink|1602|Q6727}}) {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. However, {{jalink|Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|Since the temple was abandoned in the Genwa}} years ({{jalink|1615|Q6784}}-{{jalink|1624|Q6821}}), the Tamura family of farmers took over for a while, and then Korin-ji Temple became the special charge {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}.After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in October of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), {{jalink|Modern company ranking system|Q712534}} |Q134917316}}, and was promoted to {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} on March 26, {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}13 ({{jalink|1924|Q2173}}). Furthermore, during this period, the shrine petitioned for promotion to Kokuheisha twice, once in 1908 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}) and again in 2nd year of Taisho ({{jalink|1913|Q2080}}). === God level === * August 7th, 2nd year of {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} ({{jalink|852 years|Q30383}}), the lower rank of the fifth rank (``{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905}}'') - Written as ``Shiga Riwakejin.'' === Precincts === The approach is lined with about 30 {{jalink|cherry blossom|Q871991}} tall trees. The variety is {{jalink|Azumahigan}}. The largest of these trees is 6 meters and 80 centimeters in circumference at the base, 11 meters and 5 centimeters in height, and is estimated to be about 500 years old. It is believed to be the oldest group of Higanzakura in the prefecture, and has been designated as a natural monument by Shiwa Town as the ``southern cherry tree.'' (Shigariwake-cho designated natural monument) === Setsusha === There are three shrines within the precincts as follows. . * Sakashita Inari Shrine * Onofuji Inari Shrine * Akaishi Tenmangu Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Sakashita-inari-jinja.JPG|Sakashita Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Onofuji-inari-jinja.JPG|Onofuji Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Akaishi-tenmangu.JPG|Akaishi Tenmangu Waki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Shigariwake-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Shiga Riwake Shrine'' (Shigariwake Jinja/Shikariwake Jinja, Shiga Riwake Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Iwate Prefecture|Q48326}}{{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886}}{{jalink|Shiwa Town|Q387336}} Sakuramachi. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} and {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural company}}. Also known as ``Akaishi Shrine'' and ``Ukishima Myojin''. It is known for being the northernmost Shikinai shrine. === Enshrined God === The following seven deities are enshrined. . * {{jalink|Kyotsu no Kami|Q1107286}} (Futunushi no Kami) * {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchijin}} * {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōki no Kami}} (Onamuji no Kami) * {{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona no Kami}} * {{jalink|Hoshokujin|Q2280323}} (Ukemochi no Kami) * {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahikokami}} * Funadama no Kami Among the deities enshrined, five of them, including Okami no Kami, are said to have been enshrined together in later generations. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine legend, in {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 23rd year ({{jalink|804|Q29998}}), {{jalink|Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979}} was appointed as the guardian deity of Tohoku development. . In the early part of the {{jalink|9th century|Q8083}}, the area was constructed with {{jalink|Shiva Castle|Q11491083}} ({{jalink|803 year|Q30388}}), Shiba District ({{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886}}) construction, {{jalink|Tokutan Castle|Q11489487}} construction ({ {jalink|811|Q29946}}) and other areas are being developed, and our company is also working to reward the pacification of {{jalink|Ezo|Q932574}} and to protect the area. {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. There are many examples of Shikinai-sha shrines in Ou being built by subsuming the landowner deity and inviting a central deity, but it is not clear whether this is the case for our company, and the origin of the divine name ``Shiga Riwaki'' is also uncertain {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. === Outline === The first appearance in the {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokukushi}} was in the second year of {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} ({{jalink|852 years|Q30383}}), the {{jalink|god of Shigari Wakejin}. The article states that Kana|Q11591025}} was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi, but judging from the content, it appears to have been a {{jalink|government shrine}} that had a divine rank even before Ninju {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. {{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} alink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{jalink|Shiwa District|Q1131886|Shiba District}}, it is described as "Shiga Riwake Shrine" and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1990}} is the only Shikinai shrine in Shiba County and the northernmost Shikinai shrine in Japan.After the collapse of the Ritsuryo system, there are no historical documents from the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}}, and its condition at that time is not known, but it appears to have fallen into such disrepair that one could even forget that it was a Shikinai shrine.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}} Shrine (Gods) | 1984}}, {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} At the beginning of {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}}, it was called ``Akaishi Daimyojin'' because Akaishi was {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. A legend about this red stone is that when the lord of {{jalink|Kosui-dera Castle}}, {{jalink|Akinao Shiba|Q11500879}}, passed through this area, {{jalink|Kitakami River|Q1348297}} There is a red stone at the bottom of the river, and the water waves are purple, so he wrote a poem that goes, ``Rather than Kefu, I would name it Shiwa, because the waves on the stones of this river resemble purple.''{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Because of this, the name of the district was ``Shinami-gun,'' and the name of the shrine was ``Akaishi Daimyojin,'' and it is said that the red stone that was salvaged is still enshrined within the shrine grounds.It is said that in the middle of the {{jalink|18th century|Q7015}}, the history of the Shikinai shrine was revived and the shrine building was rebuilt.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. {{jalink|Masumi Sugae, who traveled here in 1785|Q7756}} in {{jalink|Tenmei|Q1194121}} |Q11619285}}'s travelogue also mentions our company as Shigariwake Shrine, a Shikinai shrine {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. In addition, our company was revered by the {{jalink|Nambu clan|Q2975330}}, the lord of the {{jalink|Morioka clan|Q908854}}, and was dedicated with the name ``Nanbu Ichi no Miya'' {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. Early modern rituals were once carried out by Akaishiyama Hensho-ji Temple as {{jalink|Bettoji|Q11396755}}, and this Hensho-ji Temple had a temple territory of {{jalink|Keicho |Q1328185}} 30 koku were given in 7 years ({{jalink|1602|Q6727}}) {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. However, {{jalink|Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|Since the temple was abandoned in the Genwa}} years ({{jalink|1615|Q6784}}-{{jalink|1624|Q6821}}), the Tamura family of farmers took over for a while, and then Korin-ji Temple became the special charge {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}.After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in October of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}), {{jalink|Modern company ranking system|Q712534}} |Q134917316}}, and was promoted to {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} on March 26, {{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}13 ({{jalink|1924|Q2173}}). Furthermore, during this period, the shrine petitioned for promotion to Kokuheisha twice, once in 1908 ({{jalink|1908|Q2056}}) and again in 2nd year of Taisho ({{jalink|1913|Q2080}}). === God level === * August 7th, 2nd year of {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} ({{jalink|852 years|Q30383}}), the lower rank of the fifth rank (``{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905}}'') - Written as ``Shiga Riwakejin.'' === Precincts === The approach is lined with about 30 {{jalink|cherry blossom|Q871991}} tall trees. The variety is {{jalink|Azumahigan}}. The largest of these trees is 6 meters and 80 centimeters in circumference at the base, 11 meters and 5 centimeters in height, and is estimated to be about 500 years old. It is believed to be the oldest group of Higanzakura in the prefecture, and has been designated as a natural monument by Shiwa Town as the ``southern cherry tree.'' (Shigariwake-cho designated natural monument) === Setsusha === There are three shrines within the precincts as follows. . * Sakashita Inari Shrine * Onofuji Inari Shrine * Akaishi Tenmangu Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Sakashita-inari-jinja.JPG|Sakashita Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Onofuji-inari-jinja.JPG|Onofuji Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Akaishi-tenmangu.JPG|Akaishi Tenmangu Waki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Shigariwake-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Shiga Riwake Shrine'' (Shigariwake Jinja/Shikariwake Jinja, Shiga Riwake Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa Town|Q387336|WD=Q387336}} Sakuramachi. {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill||lt=Prefectural company|UNKNOWN}}. Also known as ``Akaishi Shrine'' and ``Ukishima Myojin''. It is known for being the northernmost Shikinai shrine. === Enshrined God === The following seven deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Kyotsu no Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} (Futunushi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchijin}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōki no Kami}} (Onamuji no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshokujin|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}} (Ukemochi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahikokami}} * Funadama no Kami Among the deities enshrined, five of them, including Okami no Kami, are said to have been enshrined together in later generations. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine legend, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 23rd year ({{ill|lt=804|Q29998|WD=Q29998}}), {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} was appointed as the guardian deity of Tohoku development. . In the early part of the {{ill|lt=9th century|Q8083|WD=Q8083}}, the area was constructed with {{ill|lt=Shiva Castle|Q11491083|WD=Q11491083}} ({{ill|lt=803 year|Q30388|WD=Q30388}}), Shiba District ({{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}) construction, {{ill|lt=Tokutan Castle|Q11489487|WD=Q11489487}} construction ({ {jalink|811|Q29946}}) and other areas are being developed, and our company is also working to reward the pacification of {{ill|lt=Ezo|Q932574|WD=Q932574}} and to protect the area. {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. There are many examples of Shikinai-sha shrines in Ou being built by subsuming the landowner deity and inviting a central deity, but it is not clear whether this is the case for our company, and the origin of the divine name ``Shiga Riwaki'' is also uncertain {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. === Outline === The first appearance in the {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi}} was in the second year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the {{jalink|god of Shigari Wakejin}. The article states that Kana|Q11591025}} was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi, but judging from the content, it appears to have been a {{ill||lt=government shrine|UNKNOWN}} that had a divine rank even before Ninju {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} alink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiba District}}, it is described as "Shiga Riwake Shrine" and is listed as {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1990}} is the only Shikinai shrine in Shiba County and the northernmost Shikinai shrine in Japan.After the collapse of the Ritsuryo system, there are no historical documents from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, and its condition at that time is not known, but it appears to have fallen into such disrepair that one could even forget that it was a Shikinai shrine.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}} Shrine (Gods) | 1984}}, {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} At the beginning of {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}, it was called ``Akaishi Daimyojin'' because Akaishi was {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. A legend about this red stone is that when the lord of {{ill||lt=Kosui-dera Castle|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Akinao Shiba|Q11500879|WD=Q11500879}}, passed through this area, {{ill|lt=Kitakami River|Q1348297|WD=Q1348297}} There is a red stone at the bottom of the river, and the water waves are purple, so he wrote a poem that goes, ``Rather than Kefu, I would name it Shiwa, because the waves on the stones of this river resemble purple.''{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Because of this, the name of the district was ``Shinami-gun,'' and the name of the shrine was ``Akaishi Daimyojin,'' and it is said that the red stone that was salvaged is still enshrined within the shrine grounds.It is said that in the middle of the {{ill|lt=18th century|Q7015|WD=Q7015}}, the history of the Shikinai shrine was revived and the shrine building was rebuilt.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. {{ill|lt=Masumi Sugae, who traveled here in 1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} in {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} |Q11619285}}'s travelogue also mentions our company as Shigariwake Shrine, a Shikinai shrine {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. In addition, our company was revered by the {{ill|lt=Nambu clan|Q2975330|WD=Q2975330}}, the lord of the {{ill|lt=Morioka clan|Q908854|WD=Q908854}}, and was dedicated with the name ``Nanbu Ichi no Miya'' {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. Early modern rituals were once carried out by Akaishiyama Hensho-ji Temple as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and this Hensho-ji Temple had a temple territory of {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 30 koku were given in 7 years ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}}) {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. However, {{ill|lt=Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Since the temple was abandoned in the Genwa}} years ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821}}), the Tamura family of farmers took over for a while, and then Korin-ji Temple became the special charge {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}.After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in October of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} |Q134917316}}, and was promoted to {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} on March 26, {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}13 ({{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}}). Furthermore, during this period, the shrine petitioned for promotion to Kokuheisha twice, once in 1908 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}) and again in 2nd year of Taisho ({{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080}}). === God level === * August 7th, 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the lower rank of the fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - Written as ``Shiga Riwakejin.'' === Precincts === The approach is lined with about 30 {{ill|lt=cherry blossom|Q871991|WD=Q871991}} tall trees. The variety is {{ill||lt=Azumahigan|UNKNOWN}}. The largest of these trees is 6 meters and 80 centimeters in circumference at the base, 11 meters and 5 centimeters in height, and is estimated to be about 500 years old. It is believed to be the oldest group of Higanzakura in the prefecture, and has been designated as a natural monument by Shiwa Town as the ``southern cherry tree.'' (Shigariwake-cho designated natural monument) === Setsusha === There are three shrines within the precincts as follows. . * Sakashita Inari Shrine * Onofuji Inari Shrine * Akaishi Tenmangu Shrine Waki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Shigariwake-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Shiga Riwake Shrine'' (Shigariwake Jinja/Shikariwake Jinja, Shiga Riwake Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa Town|Q387336|WD=Q387336}} Sakuramachi. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|UNKNOWN}}. Also known as ``Akaishi Shrine'' and ``Ukishima Myojin''. It is known for being the northernmost Shikinai shrine. === Enshrined God === The following seven deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Kyotsu no Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} (Futunushi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchijin}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōki no Kami}} (Onamuji no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshokujin|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}} (Ukemochi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahikokami}} * Funadama no Kami Among the deities enshrined, five of them, including Okami no Kami, are said to have been enshrined together in later generations. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine legend, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 23rd year ({{ill|lt=804|Q29998|WD=Q29998}}), {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} was appointed as the guardian deity of Tohoku development. . In the early part of the {{ill|lt=9th century|Q8083|WD=Q8083}}, the area was constructed with {{ill|lt=Shiva Castle|Q11491083|WD=Q11491083}} ({{ill|lt=803 year|Q30388|WD=Q30388}}), Shiba District ({{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}) construction, {{ill|lt=Tokutan Castle|Q11489487|WD=Q11489487}} construction ({ {jalink|811|Q29946}}) and other areas are being developed, and our company is also working to reward the pacification of {{ill|lt=Ezo|Q932574|WD=Q932574}} and to protect the area. {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. There are many examples of Shikinai-sha shrines in Ou being built by subsuming the landowner deity and inviting a central deity, but it is not clear whether this is the case for our company, and the origin of the divine name ``Shiga Riwaki'' is also uncertain {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. === Outline === The first appearance in the {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi}} was in the second year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the {{jalink|god of Shigari Wakejin}. The article states that Kana|Q11591025}} was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi, but judging from the content, it appears to have been a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=government shrine|UNKNOWN}} that had a divine rank even before Ninju {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} alink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiba District}}, it is described as "Shiga Riwake Shrine" and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1990}} is the only Shikinai shrine in Shiba County and the northernmost Shikinai shrine in Japan.After the collapse of the Ritsuryo system, there are no historical documents from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, and its condition at that time is not known, but it appears to have fallen into such disrepair that one could even forget that it was a Shikinai shrine.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}} Shrine (Gods) | 1984}}, {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} At the beginning of {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}, it was called ``Akaishi Daimyojin'' because Akaishi was {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. A legend about this red stone is that when the lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kosui-dera Castle|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Akinao Shiba|Q11500879|WD=Q11500879}}, passed through this area, {{ill|lt=Kitakami River|Q1348297|WD=Q1348297}} There is a red stone at the bottom of the river, and the water waves are purple, so he wrote a poem that goes, ``Rather than Kefu, I would name it Shiwa, because the waves on the stones of this river resemble purple.''{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Because of this, the name of the district was ``Shinami-gun,'' and the name of the shrine was ``Akaishi Daimyojin,'' and it is said that the red stone that was salvaged is still enshrined within the shrine grounds.It is said that in the middle of the {{ill|lt=18th century|Q7015|WD=Q7015}}, the history of the Shikinai shrine was revived and the shrine building was rebuilt.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. {{ill|lt=Masumi Sugae, who traveled here in 1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} in {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} |Q11619285}}'s travelogue also mentions our company as Shigariwake Shrine, a Shikinai shrine {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. In addition, our company was revered by the {{ill|lt=Nambu clan|Q2975330|WD=Q2975330}}, the lord of the {{ill|lt=Morioka clan|Q908854|WD=Q908854}}, and was dedicated with the name ``Nanbu Ichi no Miya'' {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. Early modern rituals were once carried out by Akaishiyama Hensho-ji Temple as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and this Hensho-ji Temple had a temple territory of {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 30 koku were given in 7 years ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}}) {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. However, {{ill|lt=Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Since the temple was abandoned in the Genwa}} years ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821}}), the Tamura family of farmers took over for a while, and then Korin-ji Temple became the special charge {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}.After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in October of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} |Q134917316}}, and was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} on March 26, {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}13 ({{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}}). Furthermore, during this period, the shrine petitioned for promotion to Kokuheisha twice, once in 1908 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}) and again in 2nd year of Taisho ({{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080}}). === God level === * August 7th, 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the lower rank of the fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - Written as ``Shiga Riwakejin.'' === Precincts === The approach is lined with about 30 {{ill|lt=cherry blossom|Q871991|WD=Q871991}} tall trees. The variety is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Azumahigan|UNKNOWN}}. The largest of these trees is 6 meters and 80 centimeters in circumference at the base, 11 meters and 5 centimeters in height, and is estimated to be about 500 years old. It is believed to be the oldest group of Higanzakura in the prefecture, and has been designated as a natural monument by Shiwa Town as the ``southern cherry tree.'' File:Shigariwake-jinja honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja haiden.JPG|House of worship File:Shigariwake-jinja Akaishi.JPG|Akaishi File:Shigariwake-jinja sakura.JPG|Cherry blossoms on the approach
(Shigariwake-cho designated natural monument) === Setsusha === There are three shrines within the precincts as follows. . * Sakashita Inari Shrine * Onofuji Inari Shrine * Akaishi Tenmangu Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Sakashita-inari-jinja.JPG|Sakashita Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Onofuji-inari-jinja.JPG|Onofuji Inari Shrine File:Shigariwake-jinja Akaishi-tenmangu.JPG|Akaishi Tenmangu === Festival === * Donto Festival (January 15th)The description of the festival is based on the shrine history book. *Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Prayer Festival (Spring Festival) (April 29th) * Summer Purification Ceremony (June 30th) * Doll Festival (June 30th) * Reitaisai (Autumn Festival) (first Friday, Saturday and Sunday of September) === Cultural assets === === Shiwa Town Designated Waki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Shigariwake-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Shiga Riwake Shrine'' (Shigariwake Jinja/Shikariwake Jinja, Shiga Riwake Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa Town|Q387336|WD=Q387336}} Sakuramachi. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. Also known as ``Akaishi Shrine'' and ``Ukishima Myojin''. It is known for being the northernmost Shikinai shrine. === Enshrined God === The following seven deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Kyotsu no Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} (Futunushi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchijin}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōki no Kami}} (Onamuji no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshokujin|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}} (Ukemochi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahikokami}} * Funadama no Kami Among the deities enshrined, five of them, including Okami no Kami, are said to have been enshrined together in later generations. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine legend, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 23rd year ({{ill|lt=804|Q29998|WD=Q29998}}), {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} was appointed as the guardian deity of Tohoku development. . In the early part of the {{ill|lt=9th century|Q8083|WD=Q8083}}, the area was constructed with {{ill|lt=Shiva Castle|Q11491083|WD=Q11491083}} ({{ill|lt=803 year|Q30388|WD=Q30388}}), Shiba District ({{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}) construction, {{ill|lt=Tokutan Castle|Q11489487|WD=Q11489487}} construction ({ {jalink|811|Q29946}}) and other areas are being developed, and our company is also working to reward the pacification of {{ill|lt=Ezo|Q932574|WD=Q932574}} and to protect the area. {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. There are many examples of Shikinai-sha shrines in Ou being built by subsuming the landowner deity and inviting a central deity, but it is not clear whether this is the case for our company, and the origin of the divine name ``Shiga Riwaki'' is also uncertain {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. === Outline === The first appearance in the {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi}} was in the second year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the {{jalink|god of Shigari Wakejin}. The article states that Kana|Q11591025}} was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi, but judging from the content, it appears to have been a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=government shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} that had a divine rank even before Ninju {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} alink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiba District}}, it is described as "Shiga Riwake Shrine" and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1990}} is the only Shikinai shrine in Shiba County and the northernmost Shikinai shrine in Japan.After the collapse of the Ritsuryo system, there are no historical documents from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, and its condition at that time is not known, but it appears to have fallen into such disrepair that one could even forget that it was a Shikinai shrine.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}} Shrine (Gods) | 1984}}, {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} At the beginning of {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}, it was called ``Akaishi Daimyojin'' because Akaishi was {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. A legend about this red stone is that when the lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kosui-dera Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Akinao Shiba|Q11500879|WD=Q11500879}}, passed through this area, {{ill|lt=Kitakami River|Q1348297|WD=Q1348297}} There is a red stone at the bottom of the river, and the water waves are purple, so he wrote a poem that goes, ``Rather than Kefu, I would name it Shiwa, because the waves on the stones of this river resemble purple.''{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Because of this, the name of the district was ``Shinami-gun,'' and the name of the shrine was ``Akaishi Daimyojin,'' and it is said that the red stone that was salvaged is still enshrined within the shrine grounds.It is said that in the middle of the {{ill|lt=18th century|Q7015|WD=Q7015}}, the history of the Shikinai shrine was revived and the shrine building was rebuilt.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. {{ill|lt=Masumi Sugae, who traveled here in 1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} in {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} |Q11619285}}'s travelogue also mentions our company as Shigariwake Shrine, a Shikinai shrine {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. In addition, our company was revered by the {{ill|lt=Nambu clan|Q2975330|WD=Q2975330}}, the lord of the {{ill|lt=Morioka clan|Q908854|WD=Q908854}}, and was dedicated with the name ``Nanbu Ichi no Miya'' {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. Early modern rituals were once carried out by Akaishiyama Hensho-ji Temple as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and this Hensho-ji Temple had a temple territory of {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 30 koku were given in 7 years ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}}) {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. However, {{ill|lt=Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Since the temple was abandoned in the Genwa}} years ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821}}), the Tamura family of farmers took over for a while, and then Korin-ji Temple became the special charge {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}.After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in October of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} |Q134917316}}, and was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} on March 26, {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}13 ({{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}}). Furthermore, during this period, the shrine petitioned for promotion to Kokuheisha twice, once in 1908 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}) and again in 2nd year of Taisho ({{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080}}). === God level === * August 7th, 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the lower rank of the fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - Written as ``Shiga Riwakejin.'' === Precincts === The approach is lined with about 30 {{ill|lt=cherry blossom|Q871991|WD=Q871991}} tall trees. The variety is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Azumahigan|1=UNKNOWN}}. The largest of these trees is 6 meters and 80 centimeters in circumference at the base, 11 meters and 5 centimeters in height, and is estimated to be about 500 years old. It is believed to be the oldest group of Higanzakura in the prefecture, and has been designated as a natural monument by Shiwa Town as the ``southern cherry tree.'' (Shigariwake-cho designated natural monument) === Setsusha === There are three shrines within the precincts as follows. . * Sakashita Inari Shrine * Onofuji Inari Shrine * Akaishi Tenmangu Shrine Waki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Shigariwake-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Shiga Riwake Shrine'' (Shigariwake Jinja/Shikariwake Jinja, Shiga Riwake Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa Town|Q387336|WD=Q387336}} Sakuramachi. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. Also known as ``Akaishi Shrine'' and ``Ukishima Myojin''. It is known for being the northernmost Shikinai shrine. === Enshrined God === The following seven deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Kyotsu no Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} (Futunushi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchijin}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōki no Kami}} (Onamuji no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshokujin|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}} (Ukemochi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahikokami}} * Funadama no Kami Among the deities enshrined, five of them, including Okami no Kami, are said to have been enshrined together in later generations. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine legend, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 23rd year ({{ill|lt=804|Q29998|WD=Q29998}}), {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} was appointed as the guardian deity of Tohoku development. . In the early part of the {{ill|lt=9th century|Q8083|WD=Q8083}}, the area was constructed with {{ill|lt=Shiva Castle|Q11491083|WD=Q11491083}} ({{ill|lt=803 year|Q30388|WD=Q30388}}), Shiba District ({{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}) construction, {{ill|lt=Tokutan Castle|Q11489487|WD=Q11489487}} construction ({ {jalink|811|Q29946}}) and other areas are being developed, and our company is also working to reward the pacification of {{ill|lt=Ezo|Q932574|WD=Q932574}} and to protect the area. {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. There are many examples of Shikinai-sha shrines in Ou being built by subsuming the landowner deity and inviting a central deity, but it is not clear whether this is the case for our company, and the origin of the divine name ``Shiga Riwaki'' is also uncertain {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. === Outline === The first appearance in the {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi}} was in the second year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the {{jalink|god of Shigari Wakejin}. The article states that Kana|Q11591025}} was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi, but judging from the content, it appears to have been a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=government shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} that had a divine rank even before Ninju {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} alink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiba District}}, it is described as "Shiga Riwake Shrine" and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1990}} is the only Shikinai shrine in Shiba County and the northernmost Shikinai shrine in Japan.After the collapse of the Ritsuryo system, there are no historical documents from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, and its condition at that time is not known, but it appears to have fallen into such disrepair that one could even forget that it was a Shikinai shrine.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}} Shrine (Gods) | 1984}}, {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} At the beginning of {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}, it was called ``Akaishi Daimyojin'' because Akaishi was {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. A legend about this red stone is that when the lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kosui-dera Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Akinao Shiba|Q11500879|WD=Q11500879}}, passed through this area, {{ill|lt=Kitakami River|Q1348297|WD=Q1348297}} There is a red stone at the bottom of the river, and the water waves are purple, so he wrote a poem that goes, ``Rather than Kefu, I would name it Shiwa, because the waves on the stones of this river resemble purple.''{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Because of this, the name of the district was ``Shinami-gun,'' and the name of the shrine was ``Akaishi Daimyojin,'' and it is said that the red stone that was salvaged is still enshrined within the shrine grounds.It is said that in the middle of the {{ill|lt=18th century|Q7015|WD=Q7015}}, the history of the Shikinai shrine was revived and the shrine building was rebuilt.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. {{ill|lt=Masumi Sugae, who traveled here in 1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} in {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} |Q11619285}}'s travelogue also mentions our company as Shigariwake Shrine, a Shikinai shrine {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. In addition, our company was revered by the {{ill|lt=Nambu clan|Q2975330|WD=Q2975330}}, the lord of the {{ill|lt=Morioka clan|Q908854|WD=Q908854}}, and was dedicated with the name ``Nanbu Ichi no Miya'' {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. Early modern rituals were once carried out by Akaishiyama Hensho-ji Temple as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and this Hensho-ji Temple had a temple territory of {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 30 koku were given in 7 years ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}}) {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. However, {{ill|lt=Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Since the temple was abandoned in the Genwa}} years ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821}}), the Tamura family of farmers took over for a while, and then Korin-ji Temple became the special charge {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}.After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in October of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} |Q134917316}}, and was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} on March 26, {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}13 ({{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}}). Furthermore, during this period, the shrine petitioned for promotion to Kokuheisha twice, once in 1908 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}) and again in 2nd year of Taisho ({{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080}}). === God level === * August 7th, 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the lower rank of the fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - Written as ``Shiga Riwakejin.'' === Precincts === The approach is lined with about 30 {{ill|lt=cherry blossom|Q871991|WD=Q871991}} tall trees. The variety is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Azumahigan|1=UNKNOWN}}. The largest of these trees is 6 meters and 80 centimeters in circumference at the base, 11 meters and 5 centimeters in height, and is estimated to be about 500 years old. It is believed to be the oldest group of Higanzakura in the prefecture, and has been designated as a natural monument by Shiwa Town as the ``southern cherry tree.'' (Shigariwake-cho designated natural monument) === Setsusha === There are three shrines within the precincts as follows. . * Sakashita Inari Shrine * Onofuji Inari Shrine * Akaishi Tenmangu Shrine Waki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Shigariwake-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Shiga Riwake Shrine'' (Shigariwake Jinja/Shikariwake Jinja, Shiga Riwake Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Iwate Prefecture|Q48326|WD=Q48326}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa Town|Q387336|WD=Q387336}} Sakuramachi. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=UNKNOWN}}. Also known as ``Akaishi Shrine'' and ``Ukishima Myojin''. It is known for being the northernmost Shikinai shrine. === Enshrined God === The following seven deities are enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Kyotsu no Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} (Futunushi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchijin}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōki no Kami}} (Onamuji no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Kami}} * {{ill|lt=Hoshokujin|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}} (Ukemochi no Kami) * {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahikokami}} * Funadama no Kami Among the deities enshrined, five of them, including Okami no Kami, are said to have been enshrined together in later generations. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine legend, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 23rd year ({{ill|lt=804|Q29998|WD=Q29998}}), {{ill|lt=Sakanoue Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979}} was appointed as the guardian deity of Tohoku development. . In the early part of the {{ill|lt=9th century|Q8083|WD=Q8083}}, the area was constructed with {{ill|lt=Shiva Castle|Q11491083|WD=Q11491083}} ({{ill|lt=803 year|Q30388|WD=Q30388}}), Shiba District ({{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886}}) construction, {{ill|lt=Tokutan Castle|Q11489487|WD=Q11489487}} construction ({ {jalink|811|Q29946}}) and other areas are being developed, and our company is also working to reward the pacification of {{ill|lt=Ezo|Q932574|WD=Q932574}} and to protect the area. {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. There are many examples of Shikinai-sha shrines in Ou being built by subsuming the landowner deity and inviting a central deity, but it is not clear whether this is the case for our company, and the origin of the divine name ``Shiga Riwaki'' is also uncertain {{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. === Outline === The first appearance in the {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi}} was in the second year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the {{jalink|god of Shigari Wakejin}. The article states that Kana|Q11591025}} was promoted to the lower rank of Shogoi, but judging from the content, it appears to have been a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=government shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} that had a divine rank even before Ninju {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} alink|Mutsu Province|Q907389}}{{ill|lt=Shiwa District|Q1131886|WD=Q1131886|lt=Shiba District}}, it is described as "Shiga Riwake Shrine" and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|Shiga Riwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha) | 1990}} is the only Shikinai shrine in Shiba County and the northernmost Shikinai shrine in Japan.After the collapse of the Ritsuryo system, there are no historical documents from the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}, and its condition at that time is not known, but it appears to have fallen into such disrepair that one could even forget that it was a Shikinai shrine.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}} Shrine (Gods) | 1984}}, {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} At the beginning of {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}, it was called ``Akaishi Daimyojin'' because Akaishi was {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. A legend about this red stone is that when the lord of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kosui-dera Castle|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Akinao Shiba|Q11500879|WD=Q11500879}}, passed through this area, {{ill|lt=Kitakami River|Q1348297|WD=Q1348297}} There is a red stone at the bottom of the river, and the water waves are purple, so he wrote a poem that goes, ``Rather than Kefu, I would name it Shiwa, because the waves on the stones of this river resemble purple.''{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. Because of this, the name of the district was ``Shinami-gun,'' and the name of the shrine was ``Akaishi Daimyojin,'' and it is said that the red stone that was salvaged is still enshrined within the shrine grounds.It is said that in the middle of the {{ill|lt=18th century|Q7015|WD=Q7015}}, the history of the Shikinai shrine was revived and the shrine building was rebuilt.{{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. {{ill|lt=Masumi Sugae, who traveled here in 1785|Q7756|WD=Q7756}} in {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} |Q11619285}}'s travelogue also mentions our company as Shigariwake Shrine, a Shikinai shrine {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. In addition, our company was revered by the {{ill|lt=Nambu clan|Q2975330|WD=Q2975330}}, the lord of the {{ill|lt=Morioka clan|Q908854|WD=Q908854}}, and was dedicated with the name ``Nanbu Ichi no Miya'' {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Gods)|1984}}. Early modern rituals were once carried out by Akaishiyama Hensho-ji Temple as {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, and this Hensho-ji Temple had a temple territory of {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 30 koku were given in 7 years ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}}) {{Sfn|Shigariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}. However, {{ill|lt=Motowa (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Since the temple was abandoned in the Genwa}} years ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821}}), the Tamura family of farmers took over for a while, and then Korin-ji Temple became the special charge {{Sfn|Shikariwake Shrine (Heibonsha)|1990}}.After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in October of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} |Q134917316}}, and was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} on March 26, {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}13 ({{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173}}). Furthermore, during this period, the shrine petitioned for promotion to Kokuheisha twice, once in 1908 ({{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}}) and again in 2nd year of Taisho ({{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|WD=Q2080}}). === God level === * August 7th, 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383}}), the lower rank of the fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}'') - Written as ``Shiga Riwakejin.'' === Precincts === The approach is lined with about 30 {{ill|lt=cherry blossom|Q871991|WD=Q871991}} tall trees. The variety is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Azumahigan|1=UNKNOWN}}. The largest of these trees is 6 meters and 80 centimeters in circumference at the base, 11 meters and 5 centimeters in height, and is estimated to be about 500 years old. It is believed to be the oldest group of Higanzakura in the prefecture, and has been designated as a natural monument by Shiwa Town as the ``southern cherry tree.'' (Shigariwake-cho designated natural monument) === Setsusha === There are three shrines within the precincts as follows. . * Sakashita Inari Shrine * Onofuji Inari Shrine * Akaishi Tenmangu Shrine
